title: Intensely Rich Jamaican Potatoes Gratin
description: A rich and creamy Jamaican dish featuring potatoes, infused with Jamaican jerk seasoning, perfect for a date night, and friendly to those who dislike onion and garlic.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced potatoes and jerk seasoning, and toss to coat.
In a large saucepan, heat the infused oil over medium heat.
Add the potato slices to the saucepan and cook for 5 minutes on each side, until they are slightly tender.
In a separate saucepan, heat the heavy cream over low heat until it is warm and slightly simmering.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes.
Pour some of the warm heavy cream over the potatoes, then sprinkle with some of the grated cheddar cheese.
Repeat the layers of potatoes, cream, and cheese until all of the ingredients are used, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Season with salt, black pepper, and chopped fresh thyme.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
